MELItem.Title: Cabin Air Compressor Inlet Ice Protection Systems (CIPS)

ListItem.Para: Airport OAT remains at or above -35 degrees C since last landing.
Repairs are made within 30 calendar days.

Note.Para: For an air conditioning pack to be operating normally, certain items associated with that system must operate normally. See MEL item 21-51-01 for a list of those items.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative cabin air compressor inlet ice protection system (AMM DDG 30-22-01).

ListItem.Para: For the left CIPS inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker CAC ICE PROTECT-L and lock with an INOP tag.
For the right CIPS inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker CAC ICE PROTECT-R and lock with an INOP tag.
The ANTI-ICE PACKS advisory message may be displayed.

For EICAS TAT indication blank, manually enter OAT on the Thrust Limit page of the FMC. Update OAT and recalculate V-speeds if conditions change before dispatch. Normal EICAS TAT indication will return as airspeed increases above 50 knots.

ICE DETECTORS advisory, ICE DETECTOR L/R status and WING ANTI-ICE SYS status messages may be displayed, but should clear when OAT is entered manually. If messages remain displayed, the faults must be addressed or the airplane must be dispatched per the applicable MEL item.
Opposite air conditioning pack operates normally.
Airport OAT remains at or above -35 degrees C since last landing.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One may be inoperative for one flight to the Mainbase provided:

ListItem.Para: Airplane is not operated in known or forecast icing.
Airport OAT remains at or above -35 degrees C since last landing
Flight remains within 120 minutes of landing at a suitable airport.
